title: Metric Bulk Import via Flatfile Integration
story: As a DataTools Pro user with an existing metric dictionary, I want to upload a CSV or Excel file and map its columns to metric fields, so that I can bulk-import metrics without manual data entry.
description:
Integrates the Flatfile platform for bulk metric import. Users click an import button on the Metrics Glossary page, which launches the Flatfile embedded importer. The importer handles file upload (CSV/Excel), column mapping to the metrics schema, and validation. Validated records are then inserted into the metrics database tables.

The integration maps imported columns to: Metric Name (alias), Description, Line of Business, Topic, and other metric fields. Taxonomy values (Line of Business, Topic) are created or matched during import. Validation includes:
- Name is required and must be a string
- Special characters limited to: - , . '
- Name cannot be empty

Initially explored Flatfile's hosted platform, then evaluated Flatirons Fuse as a pure JS alternative that runs entirely in-page. Worked around CSP inline script issues when embedding in the Laravel app.
acceptance_criteria: Import button on the Metrics Glossary page launches the Flatfile embedded importer
User can upload CSV or Excel files
Column mapping interface allows mapping source columns to metric fields
Metric name validation: required, string only, limited special characters (- , . ')
Imported metrics are created in the database with aliases and taxonomy assignments
Line of Business and Topic values are created or matched from the import data
Delimiter support for importing multiple taxonomy values per metric
ux_changes: Import button launches Flatfile embedded importer with file upload, column mapping, and validation UI. Records are reviewed before final submission.
middleware_changes: Import processing service: validates records, creates metrics, creates or matches taxonomy values, and establishes metric-taxonomy assignments in a single transaction.
api_changes: POST endpoint receiving validated import data from Flatfile, creating metrics with aliases and taxonomy assignments.
implementation_notes: Initially used Flatfile platform (required workspace/workbook setup via their portal). Switched to evaluating Flatirons Fuse as a pure JS alternative. Encountered CSP inline script issues when embedding in Laravel. Validation added for name field with restricted special characters. Taxonomy mapping during import handles both existing and new values.
comment_summary: Extensive integration effort — developer learned the Flatfile platform, hit workspace creation issues, evaluated Flatirons Fuse as an alternative. CSP issues resolved. Validation for name fields and taxonomy delimiter support discussed. Deferred past initial production release per request.
